---
title: ""
toc: false
sidebar: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero-light}

[{{< var course.number >}} · Graduate Course]{.hero-eyebrow}

# {{< var course.title >}} {.hero-title}

[{{< var course.subtitle >}}]{.hero-subtitle}

[A graduate-level, R-driven tour of regression analysis for education researchers — from the simple linear model through ANOVA and logistic regression to the unifying **Generalized Linear Model**.]{.hero-lead}

::: {.hero-cta}
[{{< fa file-lines >}} Syllabus](syllabus.qmd){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}
[{{< fa calendar-days >}} Schedule](schedule.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary role="button"}
[{{< fa book-open >}} Lecture Notes]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}){.btn .btn-outline-primary target="_blank" role="button"}
:::

:::

::: {.grid .course-details}

::: {.g-col-4}
### Course
- {{< fa graduation-cap >}} {{< var course.level >}}
- {{< fa laptop >}} {{< var course.modality >}}
- {{< fa location-dot >}} {{< var course.location >}}
- {{< fa building-columns >}} {{< var university.name >}}
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Instructor & development
- {{< fa chalkboard-user >}} **Instructor:** {{< var instructor.name >}} *(set each term)*
- {{< fa pen-nib >}} **Developed by** {{< var developer.name >}}
- {{< fa scale-balanced >}} Open-access · CC BY-NC-SA 4.0
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Materials
- {{< fa book-open >}} [Lecture Notes]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa laptop-code >}} [Lab Manual]({{< var books.r_lab_book >}}){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa circle-question >}} [Practice Quizzes]({{< var books.practice_quizzes >}}){target="_blank"}
:::

:::

```{=html}
<figure class="learning-arc" role="group" aria-label="The Learning Arc: the course's progression from specific to general, from the t-test to the GLM, across four Parts.">
  <svg class="learning-arc-band" viewBox="0 0 1000 150" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" aria-hidden="true" focusable="false">
    <!-- the evolving regression line: a straight t-test fit (left) -> an S-curve GLM (right) -->
    <path d="M 40 100 C 170 97, 255 94, 360 89 C 480 83, 560 78, 660 58 C 765 37, 850 30, 962 27"
          fill="none" stroke="#1A2A3A" stroke-width="2.6" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.82"/>
    <!-- scatter dots: a cloud on the left, settling onto the curve on the right -->
    <g fill="#1A2A3A" opacity="0.55">
      <circle cx="72" cy="106" r="3"/><circle cx="115" cy="95" r="3"/><circle cx="165" cy="103" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="215" cy="91" r="3"/><circle cx="268" cy="98" r="3"/><circle cx="320" cy="88" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="430" cy="83" r="3"/><circle cx="520" cy="72" r="3"/><circle cx="610" cy="58" r="3"/>
      <circle cx="715" cy="42" r="3"/><circle cx="820" cy="33" r="3"/>
    </g>
    <!-- stems descending from each Part node toward its card below -->
    <g stroke-width="2.4" stroke-linecap="round">
      <line x1="125" y1="96" x2="125" y2="132" stroke="#0B3D66"/>
      <line x1="375" y1="87" x2="375" y2="132" stroke="#2C6E91"/>
      <line x1="625" y1="63" x2="625" y2="132" stroke="#3E8E8A"/>
      <line x1="875" y1="30" x2="875" y2="132" stroke="#A8431E"/>
    </g>
    <!-- the four Part nodes sitting on the curve (cool -> warm) -->
    <circle cx="125" cy="96" r="8.5" fill="#0B3D66" stroke="#FBFAF7" stroke-width="2.5"/>
    <circle cx="375" cy="87" r="8.5" fill="#2C6E91" stroke="#FBFAF7" stroke-width="2.5"/>
    <circle cx="625" cy="63" r="8.5" fill="#3E8E8A" stroke="#FBFAF7" stroke-width="2.5"/>
    <circle cx="875" cy="30" r="8.5" fill="#A8431E" stroke="#FBFAF7" stroke-width="2.5"/>
    <!-- endpoint tags -->
    <text x="46" y="143" font-family="'Libre Franklin',sans-serif" font-size="17" font-weight="600" fill="#0B3D66"><tspan font-style="italic">t</tspan>-test</text>
    <text x="906" y="20" font-family="'Libre Franklin',sans-serif" font-size="19" font-weight="700" fill="#A8431E">GLM</text>
  </svg>

  <div class="learning-arc-parts">
    <div class="lap lap-1">
      <div class="lap-head">Part I · Foundations</div>
      <ul><li>Simple Regression</li><li>ANCOVA</li></ul>
    </div>
    <div class="lap lap-2">
      <div class="lap-head">Part II · Extensions</div>
      <ul><li>ANOVA</li><li>Multiple Regression</li><li>Interactions</li><li>Nonlinear</li></ul>
    </div>
    <div class="lap lap-3">
      <div class="lap-head">Part III · Model Evaluation</div>
      <ul><li>Model Building</li><li>Diagnostics</li></ul>
    </div>
    <div class="lap lap-4">
      <div class="lap-head">Part IV · Categorical Outcomes</div>
      <ul><li>Logistic Regression</li><li>Ordinal Models</li><li>GLM</li></ul>
    </div>
  </div>

  <figcaption class="learning-arc-cap"><strong>From the <em>t</em>-test to the GLM</strong> — <em>specific&nbsp;→&nbsp;general</em></figcaption>
</figure>
```

---

## Course overview

This course adopts a **"specific to general"** pedagogical approach: it begins with simple linear regression — a model most students have already met — and progressively extends to richer models, until familiar tools like the *t*-test and ANOVA are revealed as special cases of the **Generalized Linear Model (GLM)**. Every example runs on real education data from the `regdatasets` package, using the tidyverse and easystats ecosystems, so the code works out of the box.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**How it works.** This is a fully **asynchronous, self-paced** course. Each session you **read** a Lecture Notes chapter, **work through** the matching Lab, **check yourself** with a Practice Quiz, and **submit** homework or a project milestone when due. Assessment is completion-based homework plus mastery-based quizzes and two projects — see the [Syllabus](syllabus.qmd) for grading and the [Schedule](schedule.qmd) for the full outline. **No prior R experience required** — Session 1 starts from setup.
:::

### What you will learn

1. **Specify** linear and generalized linear models with continuous and categorical predictors, interactions, and nonlinear terms.
2. **Interpret** coefficients, confidence intervals, and test statistics in substantive and statistical terms.
3. **Diagnose** violations of model assumptions and identify influential observations.
4. **Build** parsimonious models using confirmatory and exploratory strategies.
5. **Extend** regression to binary and ordinal outcomes via logistic and ordinal models.
6. **Implement** analyses in R with reproducible, well-documented code.
7. **Communicate** statistical findings clearly to diverse audiences.

## Course materials

All materials are open-access and published together as one site:

| Material | What it is |
|----------|------------|
| [**Lecture Notes**]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}){target="_blank"} | The narrative textbook — theory, math, and runnable R ({{< var materials.chapters >}} chapters) |
| [**Lab Manual**]({{< var books.r_lab_book >}}){target="_blank"} | Hands-on R tutorials paralleling the notes ({{< var materials.labs >}} labs) |
| [**Practice Quizzes**]({{< var books.practice_quizzes >}}){target="_blank"} ✨ | Interactive, ungraded self-check with instant feedback ({{< var materials.quizzes >}} quizzes) |
| [**Project Guide**]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} | Guidelines + worked examples for the {{< var materials.projects >}} course projects |
| [**regdatasets**]({{< var books.regdatasets >}}){target="_blank"} | R package bundling {{< var materials.datasets >}} curated education datasets |

::: {.cta-secondary}
[New here? Two steps to begin.]{.cta-secondary-title}

[{{< fa play >}} Start Session 1](content/01-content.qmd){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}
[{{< fa download >}} Set up R & RStudio](resource/r-setup.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary role="button"}
:::
